sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I have a clearer picture:
Initial state:
- Two green squares in left panel: [30,18]-[32,20] (W center) and [30,27]-[32,29] (B center)
- White 3x3 block at [30,30]-[32,32]
- Left frame: [32,11]-[36,15]
- Right frame: [26,44]-[30,48]
After pressing RIGHT 4 times, the board transformed:
- Purple bar appeared in the middle
- Left green square at [30,30]-[32,32]
- Right green square at [30,42]-[32,44]
After pressing LEFT 2 times:
- Right green square moved to [30,39]-[32,41]
After pressing UP:
- Right green square moved to [27,39]-[29,41] (up by 3)
- Left green square stayed at [30,30]-[32,32]
So:
- UP moves the right green square up
- DOWN probably moves the right green square down
- LEFT moves the right green square left
- RIGHT moves the left green square right
Wait, but earlier RIGHT moved the left green square. Let me reconsider. Maybe:
- UP/DOWN/LEFT control the right green square
- RIGHT controls the left green square
Or maybe the control scheme is:
- The "active" green square (the one with W center) is controlled by arrow keys
- Clicking switches which square is active
Currently the right green has W center and it moved UP. The left green has c center and didn't move. This supports the theory that arrow keys control the active (W-center) square.
So the mechanic might be:
1. Click a green square to make it active (W center)
2. Arrow keys move the active square
3. The goal is to move green squares into the frames
The right frame is at [26,44]-[30,48]. The right green square is currently at [27,39]-[29,41]. To fit inside the frame, it needs to be at approximately [27,45]-[29,47] (inside the frame's interior).
Let me try to move the right green square into the right frame. It's at [27,39]-[29,41], needs to go right to [27,45]-[29,47]. But RIGHT moves the left square, not the right one...
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe arrow keys always move the active square, regardless of which side it's on. Let me try RIGHT now to see if it moves the right green square (which is currently active with W center).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: right green is active (W center) at [27,39]-[29,41]
# Try RIGHT to see if it moves the active (right) green square
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
